document.title: Living Folder Manifest SoftSpec
document.purpose: Describe the permissive folder-local constitution used by Living Folders.

vocabulary.command: Run command from this folder after explicit trust.

map-geometry.description: Committed rectangle geometry in canvas coordinates.

map-entry-state.description: Durable reconciliation intent for one immediate filesystem entry.

vocabulary.placed: Render this entry on the map; retain it as a missing ghost if it disappears from disk.
vocabulary.ignored: Keep the entry out of both the map and Incoming tray until restored.

keys.kind: last known filesystem kind, such as file or folder
keys.visual-kind: last known visual classification used when rendering a missing ghost

map-text.description: A user-authored text annotation placed on the Directory Map.

keys.labelled-region: boolean; when true, render the text as a label interrupting the top border of a resizable empty region

vocabulary.thin: Draw the region outline one pixel thick.
vocabulary.thick: Draw the region outline two pixels thick.

keys.width: integer region width; meaningful when labelled-region is true
keys.height: integer region height; meaningful when labelled-region is true

map-image.description: A graphical image element backed by a content-addressed asset.

temporal-view.description: Folder-local choices for projecting ordinary entries onto time. The filesystem remains authoritative.

vocabulary.daystream: Arrange day nodes as one continuous Monday-first strip of weeks.

vocabulary.day: Gather entries at ISO calendar-day nodes.

vocabulary.most-recent: Initially reveal the latest occupied temporal node.
vocabulary.today: Initially reveal the current day when it is in range.
vocabulary.earliest: Initially reveal the earliest occupied node.

keys.future-context-weeks: integer from 0 through 12; number of empty following weeks retained after the latest occupied day

hidden-file-patterns.description: Entries excluded from both temporal nodes and the exceptions shelf; defaults to *~.

show-modification-associations.description: Reserved preference for exposing weaker filesystem-metadata associations; false by default.

living-folder-manifest.title: Living Folder Manifest
living-folder-manifest.description: A folder's small local constitution stored at .living-folder/description.json. Unknown keys are preserved.
rules: Omitting presentation-mode returns presentation authority to inference.
buttons contains user-declared controls; runnable files may additionally be detected from the directory.
directory-map geometry is keyed by the immediate child entry name.
directory-map images refer to content-addressed assets under .living-folder/images.
resize caches are derived artifacts under .living-folder/images/cache and are not authoritative.
Legacy .living-folder.json files are readable but deprecated; new writes use the directory form.
The constitution augments the filesystem and never replaces it.
Temporal interpretation gathers entries around dates found in names; it does not move or rewrite those entries.
Unmatched temporal entries remain visible in the exceptions shelf unless an explicit hidden-file-pattern matches them.
Directory Map entries are reconciled as placed, incoming, ignored, or missing.
Incoming is derived from immediate filesystem entries absent from entry-states; missing is derived from placed entry-states absent from the filesystem.
Removing a missing ghost removes only its map records because no filesystem entry remains to delete.

keys.title: human-facing folder title
keys.role: semantic role vocabulary; may be inferred or authored
keys.purpose: short statement of why this place exists

trust-runnable-code.description: Whether the user has explicitly trusted runnable code in this particular folder.

presentation-mode.description: Explicit presentation override such as temporal, directory-map, project-root, workbench, factory, library, gallery, inbox, vault, warehouse, hallway, or ruin.

button-order.description: Presentation order shared by declared button:<id> and detected:<filename> controls. Unknown or stale identities are ignored and newly available buttons are appended.

keys.commands: optional mapped annotations for detected runnable filenames

entry-states.description: Placed and ignored intent. Entries absent from this object appear in Incoming.

z-order.description: Back-to-front layer keys such as entry:<name>, text:<id>, and image:<id>.
